title: microsorum insigne
intro: Microsorum insigne, also known as the Polynesian Water Fern or Water Fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, including Australia, New Caledonia, and the Solomon Islands. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Microsorum insigne include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, Microsorum insigne is often used in teas, infusions, or topical applications, and its leaves are also used in traditional Polynesian medicine to create poultices and salves for skin conditions and wounds.
